skill.name: wonda-cli
skill.description: Using the Wonda CLI to generate images, videos, music, and audio from the terminal — plus LinkedIn, Reddit, and X/Twitter research and automation
skill.summary: Generate images, videos, music, and audio via terminal with built-in social media research and automation
skill.author: degausai

security_audit.summary: This is a documentation file (SKILL.md) for the Wonda CLI tool, not executable code. All 459 static findings are false positives: command examples in markdown are flagged as external command execution; URLs in documentation are flagged as hardcoded URLs; WONDA_API_KEY environment variable references are flagged as credential access. The credentials vault uses AES-256-GCM encryption (strong cryptography, not weak). The skill provides legitimate content creation and social media automation functionality.

low_findings.title: Documentation Pattern: Shell Command Examples
low_findings.description: 413 shell command examples in markdown code blocks are flagged as external command execution. These are documentation showing CLI usage, not actual command execution.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: SKILL.md is documentation for terminal-based AI agents. All flagged lines are markdown code blocks showing wonda CLI command syntax, not executable code.

low_findings.title: Documentation Pattern: Environment Variable Reference
low_findings.description: WONDA_API_KEY environment variable references are flagged as credential access. These are legitimate authentication setup instructions.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The skill documents API key authentication for the Wonda CLI. This is standard CLI authentication pattern, not a security vulnerability.

low_findings.title: Documentation Pattern: Hardcoded URLs
low_findings.description: URLs in documentation are flagged as hardcoded network references. These are legitimate reference examples for billing and social platforms.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: URLs are documentation examples for a social media CLI tool. https://app.wondercat.ai/settings/billing is a billing reference; other URLs are social platform examples.

low_findings.title: Documentation Pattern: Cryptographic References
low_findings.description: AES-256-GCM encryption references are flagged as weak cryptographic algorithms. AES-256-GCM is actually strong encryption.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The skill documents that passwords are encrypted with AES-256-GCM. This is strong, modern encryption (not weak). The static scanner incorrectly flags 'AES' as weak algorithm.

low_findings.title: Documentation Pattern: System Reconnaissance
low_findings.description: Tool discovery commands (which, fc-match, ls) are flagged as system reconnaissance. These are legitimate tool availability checks for a CLI helper skill.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Commands like 'which ffmpeg' and 'fc-match' are standard tool availability checks. A CLI helper skill needs to verify tools are installed before using them.

low_findings.title: Legitimate Feature: Credentials Vault
low_findings.description: Browser credential storage is flagged as sensitive credential access. This is the intended feature of the skill - encrypted credential storage for social media automation.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The credentials vault with AES-256-GCM encryption is a documented feature of the Wonda CLI. It stores social media login credentials securely.

content.user_title: Generate AI Images, Videos, and Music from Terminal
content.value_statement: Marketing teams and content creators spend hours on manual content production and social media research. Wonda CLI brings AI generation, editing, and social platform automation to the terminal for AI agents.
actual_capabilities: Generate images using GPT Image 2, Seedance 2, Kling, and other AI models
Create videos from text or images with Seedance 2, Sora, Kling, and Veo
Produce music with Suno AI and text-to-speech with ElevenLabs
Add animated captions, text overlays, and effects to videos
Research social platforms (LinkedIn, Reddit, X/Twitter) via CLI commands
Publish content directly to Instagram, TikTok, and social platforms
limitations: Requires Wonda account and API authentication (free or paid tier)
Video duration limited to 15s per clip (use merge for longer content)
Some features require paid plan (video analysis, skill commands)

use_cases.title: Marketing Teams: Rapid Content Production
use_cases.description: Generate product images, videos, and social posts directly from terminal workflows. Automate competitor research and content scheduling without leaving your development environment.

use_cases.title: Content Creators: Batch Content Generation
use_cases.description: Create multiple variations of images and videos, add captions and effects, and batch publish to multiple social platforms from simple CLI commands.
use_cases.target_user: Content creators and social media managers

use_cases.title: AI Agent Developers: Integrated Automation
use_cases.description: Build agents that leverage AI media generation and social platform interaction. Chain generation, editing, and publishing into automated workflows.
use_cases.target_user: AI agent developers and automation engineers

prompt_templates.title: Generate an image for social media
prompt_templates.prompt: Use wonda to generate a 9:16 portrait image of [DESCRIPTION] in [STYLE] style. Save it as output.png.
prompt_templates.scenario: Quick single image generation for social content

prompt_templates.title: Create video with animated captions
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate a 9:16 video showing [SCENE DESCRIPTION] then add animated TikTok-style captions with the text '[CAPTION TEXT]'.
prompt_templates.scenario: Video with text overlay for social platforms

prompt_templates.title: Research competitors on social platforms
prompt_templates.prompt: Use wonda to scrape Instagram posts from @competitor_handle, analyze their top performing content, and generate 3 similar post ideas.

prompt_templates.title: Batch generate and publish to social
prompt_templates.prompt: Create 5 product images using different AI styles, add text overlay to each, and publish them to Instagram as drafts with captions '[PRODUCT DESCRIPTION] - Link in bio'.
prompt_templates.scenario: Full pipeline from generation to publishing

output_examples.input: Generate a portrait image of a coffee cup in warm morning light
output_examples.output: Successfully generated image. Media ID: abc123xyz, URL: https://cdn.wondercat.ai/abc123.png (1024x1024)

output_examples.input: Create a 9:16 video of a person running and add TikTok captions
output_examples.prompt: Generate video: person running in golden hour light, style: cinematic, duration: 5s, aspect: 9:16
output_examples.output: Video generated with ID: vid789abc. Adding animated captions with TikTok Red style. Final output: https://cdn.wondercat.ai/vid789.mp4

output_examples.input: Search Reddit for trending discussions about productivity apps
output_examples.output: Found 15 trending posts in r/productivity. Top 3: (1) 'My 5-app setup that doubled my output' - 2.4k upvotes, (2) 'Notion vs Obsidian in 2025' - 1.8k upvotes, (3) 'AI writing assistants tested' - 950 upvotes

best_practices: Use --attach with reference images for consistent style across generations. For images with people, use kling_3_pro model to preserve facial identity.
Set --aspect-ratio 9:16 for all social content (TikTok, Reels, Stories). Only use landscape for YouTube or presentations.
Chain commands using media IDs instead of downloading and re-uploading. Pass --media <id> directly to subsequent operations.
anti_patterns: Do not describe image content in image-to-video prompts. Only describe motion, camera movement, and atmosphere.
Do not chain TTS + lipsync for talking-head content. Native speech video models (Sora, Veo, Kling) generate better results with mouth sync baked in.
Do not auto-select spicy/NSFW models like 'cookie'. Only use them when the user explicitly requests such content.

faq.question: What authentication do I need?
faq.answer: Run 'wonda auth login' to open browser authentication, or set the WONDA_API_KEY environment variable with your API key from app.wondercat.ai.

faq.question: What image models are available?
faq.answer: Default is GPT Image 2 for best quality. Alternatives include nano-banana-2 for multi-reference, seedream-4-5 for 3D style, and cookie for NSFW (only when explicitly requested).

faq.question: How do I add captions to videos?
faq.answer: Use 'wonda edit video --operation animatedCaptions --media <id> --params {...}' to automatically transcribe and render word-by-word animated captions.

faq.question: Can I automate social media posting?
faq.answer: Yes. Connect accounts with 'wonda accounts instagram' or 'wonda accounts tiktok', then publish with 'wonda publish instagram --media <id> --account <accountId> --caption "..."'.

faq.question: How long does video generation take?
faq.answer: Seedance 2: 2-5 min, Sora Pro: 5-10 min, Veo 3.1: 1-3 min, Kling: 3-8 min. Use --wait flag and --timeout for longer jobs.

faq.question: What is the maximum video duration?
faq.answer: Single clip: 15s for Seedance 2, 20s for Sora. For longer content, generate multiple clips and merge with 'wonda edit video --operation merge --media clip1,clip2,clip3'.
